content: Fresh ginger is dominated by gingerols, while dried ginger is predominantly shogaols.
Hot air drying ginger at 1500°C for 6 hours produces the highest concentrations of shogaols and strongest antioxidant activity.
Shogaols consistently outperform gingerols in anti-inflammatory assays, antioxidant activity, and TRPV1 receptor.
Three distinct mechanisms make ginger effective for glucose control, insulin resistance, and visceral fat.
Shogaols bind more tightly to TRPV1 channel than gingerols due to longer hydrophobic tail.
10-shogaol produced during steaming or drying achieves all of fat loss mechanisms without spicy sensation.
Fresh ginger ginger promotes white adipose tissue conversion into metabolically active brown fat.
Dry ginger activates TRPV1 and adrenaline secretion for thermogenic signaling without spicy sensation.
